title: How to Budget for a Multi-Location Move (e.g., Splitting Household Items and Moving Offices at the Same Time)
excerpt: Multi-location move budget: household and office split, storage, sequencing costs, standby, corporate and residential combined relocation planning Addis.
content:
How to budget for a multi-location move (e.g., splitting household items and moving offices at the same time)

Splitting household goods to new apartment while office FF&E goes to new suite—and maybe archive to storage—multiplies addresses, trucks, and cognitive load. Budgeting fails when you price one truck mentally but need three legs, two elevator books, and standby on ring road twice.

This guide builds a multi-location move budget for combined household and office relocations in Addis.

Cost buckets to line-item

  • Residential leg: Inventory-based home quote with stairs and shuttle.
  • Office leg: FF&E, IT vendor parallel—not hidden inside home quote.
  • Storage leg: Unit rent, access, second truck if direct office-to-storage.
  • IT and telecom: ISP, PBX, server rack—vendor invoices separate.
  • Overlap housing: Double rent or hotel nights during gap.
  • Standby buffer: Ten to fifteen percent for traffic and elevator delays.

Sequencing affects price

Same-day multi-stop one truck cheapest if routing efficient—office morning, home afternoon, storage last. Split days doubles mobilization fees but reduces chaos. Model both in spreadsheet.

Color inventory prevents rework

Mis-delivered office box to home bedroom costs return trip through Addis—budget time or forbid with strict labeling—HOME blue, OFFICE green, STORAGE yellow.

Hidden multi-location traps

  • Two compound security deposits simultaneously.
  • Duplicate packing materials if legs uncoordinated.
  • Childcare and pet board during both legs.
  • Meals and fuel for volunteers—real birr.

Corporate contribution versus personal

Employer may cover office leg only—separate vendor invoices for reimbursement clarity. Personal household on own contract—no blended audit mess.

Active Movers multi-stop quotes

Request single dispatch plan with per-leg pricing visible—transparency beats one opaque lump sum.

Contingency fund

Hold five to ten percent for overnight hold, extra shuttle, or urgent storage week—multi-location moves find edge cases.

Conclusion

Budget multi-location moves as sum of legs plus overlap plus standby—not single home quote times two. Line-item, sequence wisely, label ruthlessly—and combined household-office relocation stays finance-approved.
author: Active Movers Team
